allies.rationale_text: Decomposition gives algorithmic-thinking the clean subparts and handoffs that make a procedure reliable instead of hand-wavy.
allies.source_quote: It provides a crucial method for tackling complex tasks by systematically breaking them apart.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps algorithmic-thinking keep procedure design connected to the interrelationships and boundaries of the larger system.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking ... provides the essential framework for seeing interrelationships and the whole picture.

allies.rationale_text: Constraints help algorithmic-thinking define bounded edge cases, reliable handoffs, and the limits within which the procedure should operate.
allies.source_quote: Best used when a process benefits from explicit branching rules, bounded edge cases, and coordination across specialized components or agents.

antagonists.rationale_text: Simplification can undermine algorithmic-thinking when abstraction strips out the edge cases the procedure must still handle safely.
antagonists.source_quote: algorithmic-thinking conflicts with simplification (simplification) when abstraction removes decision-critical edge cases from the procedure.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias can get baked into the procedure when the algorithm is designed to confirm the designer's prior model rather than test it against hard cases.
antagonists.source_quote: algorithmic-thinking conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when procedure design embeds assumptions that confirm the designer's prior model rather than testing it against edge cases.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Algorithmic-thinking conflicts with simplification when abstraction removes decision-critical edge cases from the procedure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: algorithmic-thinking vs simplification: algorithmic-thinking conflicts with simplification (simplification) when abstraction removes decision-critical edge cases from the procedure.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Algorithmic-thinking conflicts with feedback-loops when static rule pipelines fail to update after signal drift.
structured_tensions.source_quote: algorithmic-thinking vs feedback-loops: algorithmic-thinking conflicts with feedback-loops (feedback loops) when static rule pipelines fail to update after signal drift.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Algorithmic-thinking conflicts with confirmation-bias when procedure design embeds assumptions that confirm the designer's prior model rather than testing it against edge cases.
structured_tensions.source_quote: algorithmic-thinking vs confirmation-bias: algorithmic-thinking conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when procedure design embeds assumptions that confirm the designer's prior model rather than testing it against edge cases.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on structural allies and an explicit tension block around simplification, drift, and designer bias, so the file preserves that bounded surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor workflow topology beyond the strongest procedural, systems, and explicit tension links.
open_questions: Feedback-loops appears as a stronger explicit tension than as a clean first-order ally in the current source shape, so it was kept there instead of being duplicated as both.
